questions.text: Li is different from its family members due to _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Lithium shows peculiar behaviour from its members. It has small radius.
High charge density is one of the anomalous behaviour of Li.
The so called anomalous properties of Li are due to the fact that Li is less electropositive than Na.

Reason: Li has a small radius and high charge density. It is also unexpectedly less electropositive than sodium. These characteristics of lithium are due to the fact that it only has 3 electrons in 2 shells. Therefore, A, B and C are correct statements. This means the answer is D.

questions.explanation: Reason: Li has a small radius and high charge density. It is also unexpectedly less electropositive than sodium. These characteristics of lithium are due to the fact that it only has 3 electrons in 2 shells. Therefore, A, B and C are correct statements. This means the answer is D.

questions.text: Which of the following oxide is formed by alkali metals?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkali metals form basic oxides. When dissolved in water, they produce hydroxide ions and hydrogen gas giving basic solution. Alkali metals are basic in nature.
Acidic oxides are formed by non-metals and form acids in water.
Amphoteric oxides show both acidic and basic properties example: BeO ZnO etc.
This is incorrect information.

questions.explanation: Alkali metals are basic. The oxides they form are also basic due to this. They react strongly with water and create hydroxide ions and hydrogen gas, leaving a basic solution.

questions.text: Which one of the following substances conduct electricity by the movement of ions?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Graphite conducts electricity via the movement of electrons within its structure, not ions.
Copper is a metal that conducts electricity through the movement of electrons, not ions.
Molten sodium chloride conducts electricity through the movement of ions, making it an electrolyte.
Mercury is a metal that conducts electricity through electron movement, not ions.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is molten sodium chloride. When sodium chloride is in its molten state, it dissociates into sodium and chloride ions, which are free to move and conduct electricity. This is characteristic of electrolytes, which conduct electricity via ion movement. Graphite (A) and metals like Copper (B) and Mercury (D) conduct electricity through the movement of electrons, not ions, which is different from the mechanism in electrolytes.

questions.text: lonization energy depends on which of the following factors?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A is incorrect since only the size of atoms is not the factor affecting ionization energy. Though there is an increase in ionization energy as the size of the atoms decreases, this factor alone cannot be used alone to predict the trend of ionization energy.

B is incorrect since the only nuclear charge is not the factor affecting ionization energy. Though there is an increase in ionization energy as the nuclear charge increases, this factor alone cannot be used alone to predict the trend of ionization energy.

C is incorrect since only the number of shells is not the factor affecting ionization energy. Though there is a decrease in ionization energy as the number of shells increases, this factor alone cannot be used alone to predict the trend of ionization energy.

options.text: All of these options are correct
options.explanation: Option D is correct since all of the options mention the correct reasons for the ionization energy trend and all three reasons have to be looked at to set up a trend of ionization energy. Option A is insufficient since only the size of atoms is not the factor affecting ionization energy. Though there is an increase in ionization energy as the size of the atoms decreases, this factor alone cannot be used alone to predict the trend of ionization energy. Option B is insufficient since the only nuclear charge is not the factor affecting ionization energy. Though there is an increase in ionization energy as the nuclear charge increases, this factor alone cannot be used alone to predict the trend of ionization energy. Option C is insufficient since only the number of shells is not the factor affecting ionization energy. Though there is a decrease in ionization energy as the number of shells increases, this factor alone cannot be used alone to predict the trend of ionization energy.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct since all of the options mention the correct reasons for the ionization energy trend and all three reasons have to be looked at to set up a trend of ionization energy. Option A is insufficient since only the size of atoms is not the factor affecting ionization energy. Though there is an increase in ionization energy as the size of the atoms decreases, this factor alone cannot be used alone to predict the trend of ionization energy. Option B is insufficient since the only nuclear charge is not the factor affecting ionization energy. Though there is an increase in ionization energy as the nuclear charge increases, this factor alone cannot be used alone to predict the trend of ionization energy. Option C is insufficient since only the number of shells is not the factor affecting ionization energy. Though there is a decrease in ionization energy as the number of shells increases, this factor alone cannot be used alone to predict the trend of ionization energy.

questions.text: The first ionization potential of alkaline earth metal is greater than alkali metals because?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because ionization potential of metals doesn't depend upon reactivity.
This option is incorrect because alkaline earth metals don't have greater atomic radii.

This option is correct because the first ionization energies of alkaline earth metals are higher than those of the alkali metals. This is because of the increase in the nuclear charge of the alkaline earth metal.
This option is incorrect because option C is the correct option thus all cannot be the correct answer.

questions.explanation: Correct option is C. Alkaline earth metals have smaller atomic sizes as compared to alkali metals so electrons of outermost shells of alkaline earth metals are held tightly. As the nuclear charge increases it requires more energy to remove electrons(Ionization energy) hence ionization energy will be increased. The first ionization energies of alkaline earth metals are higher than those of the alkali metals. This is because of the increase in the nuclear charge of the alkaline earth metal.

questions.text: Metals are _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A substance that tends to bring about reduction by being oxidized and losing electrons is reducing agentMetals lose electrons when they react and make the other reactant gain electrons (reduction) hence they are "reducing agents".
A substance that tends to bring about oxidation by being reduced and gaining electrons is oxidizing agent
IncorrectOption B: A substance that tends to bring about oxidation by being reduced and gaining electrons is oxidizing agentNot the case with metals
A dehydrating agent is a substance that dries or removes water from a material. In chemical reactions where dehydration occurs, the reacting molecule loses a molecule of water.

questions.explanation: A substance that tends to bring about reduction by being oxidized and losing electrons is reducing agentMetals lose electrons when they react and make the other reactant gain electrons (reduction) hence they are "reducing agents".

questions.text: Melting points of Group II-A elements are higher than those of Group I-A because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Atoms of II-A elements have smaller size
options.explanation: From left to right in a period, the number of sizes decreases, but it is not a suitable reason for increase in boiling point because because mettalic character decreases from left to right and we have non-metal on the right-most side
reactivity is not a factor to be considered while talking about melting and boiling points.

options.text: Atoms of II-A elements provide two binding electrons
options.explanation: Metallic character as well as melting and boiling increase until period number three because the number of free electrons available for forming a metallic bond increases, so strong metallic bonds are formed As a result, melting Melting/Boiling boiling increases. Group-IIA offers two electrons for bonding while Group-IA offers one electron for bonding so it means IIA forms a stronger metallic bond.

options.text: I-A elements have smaller atomic radius
options.explanation: size of IA is greater than IIA

questions.explanation: Metallic character as well as melting and boiling increase until period number three because the number of free electrons available for forming a metallic bond increases, so strong metallic bonds are formed As a result, melting Melting/Boiling boiling increases. Group-IIA offers two electrons for bonding while Group-IA offers one electron for bonding so it means IIA forms a stronger metallic bond.

questions.text: Melting point of Na & Mg decreases down the group due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Melting points of metals decrease down the group due to Increment in sizes. Hence, C is the correct option.
Melting points of metals decrease down the group due to Increment in sizes. Hence, C is the correct option.

Generally, the melting point of the metals decreases down the group. This is because as the metal ions get larger the distance between the bonding electrons and the positive nucleus gets larger and reduces the overall attraction between the two.
Melting points of metals decrease down the group due to Increment in sizes. Hence, C is the correct option.

questions.explanation: Generally the melting point of the metals decreases down the group. This is because as the metal ions get larger the distance between the bonding electrons and the positive nucleus gets larger and reduces the overall attraction between the two.
hints: Melting point of Na & Mg decreases down the group

questions.text: Which of the following has highest atomic size?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: a) Be (Beryllium):Beryllium has the smallest atomic size among the options. This is because, as you move from left to right across a period of the periodic table, the atomic size generally decreases due to an increase in the effective nuclear charge (protons) pulling the electrons closer to the nucleus.

b) Mg (Magnesium):Magnesium has a larger atomic size compared to beryllium but is still relatively small due to being in the same period. As you move from left to right in a period, atomic size tends to decrease.

c) Ca (Calcium):Calcium has a larger atomic size than both beryllium and magnesium. It is located further to the left in the periodic table and is in a higher period, so it has more energy levels (electron shells) which contribute to a larger atomic size.

d) Ba (Barium):Barium has the largest atomic size among the options. It is located in the same group (column) as calcium and magnesium but is further down the group. Moving down a group on the periodic table generally results in an increase in atomic size due to the addition of more energy levels.

questions.explanation: Barium has the largest atomic size among the options. It is located in the same group (column) as calcium and magnesium but is further down the group. Moving down a group on the periodic table generally results in an increase in atomic size due to the addition of more energy levels.

questions.text: Sodium reacts with water more vigorously than lithium because it:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sodium does have a higher atomic weight than lithium; however, this is not the reason for its increased reactivity with water. Reactivity in alkali metals is more influenced by electropositive character, not atomic weight. Thus, option A is incorrect.
This option is incorrect because electronegativity decreases down the group in the periodic table. Sodium, being lower in the group than lithium, is less electronegative, not more.

This is the correct option. Sodium is more electropositive than lithium, meaning it more readily loses electrons to form positive ions. This property increases down the group, making sodium react more vigorously with water.

While sodium is indeed a metal, this characteristic is shared by all alkali metals, including lithium. Thus, being a metal does not uniquely explain sodium's higher reactivity with water compared to lithium, making option D incorrect.
This option is incorrect because sodium actually has a lower melting point than lithium. Melting point is not a factor that affects the reactivity of sodium with water.

questions.explanation: Sodium reacts more vigorously with water than lithium primarily because sodium is more electropositive. Electropositivity increases as you move down a group in the periodic table, which means sodium, being below lithium, loses electrons more easily. This increased tendency to lose electrons results in a more vigorous reaction with water. Other factors such as atomic weight, electronegativity, and melting point are not the primary reasons for this difference in reactivity. All alkali metals including sodium and lithium are metals, so this characteristic is not a differentiating factor either.

questions.text: Only two elements are present in:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Period 1 contains only 2 elements: hydrogen and helium, due to the limited capacity of the first electron shell.
Period 2 contains 8 elements, as it fills both the 2s and 2p orbitals.
Period 3 also contains 8 elements, filling the 3s and 3p orbitals.
Period 4 contains 18 elements, including transition metals that fill the 3d orbitals.
Period 5 contains 18 elements, similar to Period 4, involving the filling of the 4d orbitals.

questions.explanation: Period 1 of the periodic table consists of only two elements: hydrogen and helium. This is because the first electron shell (the 1s orbital) can hold a maximum of two electrons. In contrast, Periods 2 and 3 each have 8 elements, as they include the filling of s and p orbitals. Periods 4 and 5 have even more elements, 18 each, due to the inclusion of the d orbitals. Hence, the correct answer is Period 1, where only two elements are present.

questions.text: The most dense element is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Lithium is a light metal with an atomic number of 3. It has a relatively low density, especially in comparison to some heavier elements. Its density is around 0.53 grams per cubic centimeter (g/cm³) at room temperature.
Potassium is an alkali metal with an atomic number of 19. It is a relatively light metal with a density of about 0.89 g/cm³ at room temperature.
Calcium is an alkaline earth metal with an atomic number of 20. It is denser than lithium and potassium. Calcium has a density of approximately 1.54 g/cm³ at room temperature.

The density (ratio between mass and volume as m/v) decreases from top to bottom in group 1(alkali metals) as the size increases and the nuclear charge decreases. In periods, density increases from left to right as atom size decreases and the nuclear charge increases. The alkaline earth metals are denser than the alkali metals due to their smaller size. The densities of alkaline earth metals do not show any regular trend with increasing atomic numbers. The density of these metal first decreases from Be to Ca and then increases from Ca to Ba. The trend of density amongst alkali metals: Li<K<Na<Rb<Cs The trend of density amongst alkaline earth metals: Ca<Mg<Be<Sr<Ba<Ra Hence, in this case, Ba is the densest element out of all the given options.
Atomic number: 37 Atomic mass: ~85.47 g/mol Rubidium is another alkali metal, and while it is heavier than lithium and potassium, it is still relatively light compared to some other elements.

questions.explanation: Down the group, the density of the elements increases, and Group II elements have a higher density than Group I elements because of their higher mass and lesser volumes. Ba is the only group II in all the options above.

questions.text: Potassium, Rubidium, Cesium react with oxygen to form which types of oxides?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Under typical conditions, alkali metals react with oxygen to form normal oxides.Example: 2K + O2 → 2K2O
Under more extreme conditions (higher temperatures or increased pressure), alkali metals can form superoxides.Example: 2K + O2 → 2KO2
With a limited supply of oxygen, alkali metals can also form peroxides.Example: 2K + O2 → 2K2O2
Suboxides, where the metal is in a lower oxidation state than in the normal oxide, are less common.Example: The suboxide of potassium is K4O9.

questions.explanation: Li reacts with oxygen to form normal oxide. Na reacts with oxygen to make peroxide. Potassium, Rubidium and Caesium react with oxygen to form Superoxide.

questions.text: Magnesium reacts with nitrogen to form:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This compound indicates that two magnesium atoms react with two nitrogen atoms. The correct formula for the compound formed by magnesium and nitrogen is magnesium nitride, and its correct formula is Mg3N2.
This is the correct compound formed by the reaction of three magnesium atoms with two nitrogen atoms. The correct name for this compound is magnesium nitride.
This option suggests a compound formed by one magnesium atom and two nitrogen atoms. However, the correct formula for the compound formed by magnesium and nitrogen is Mg3N2.
This indicates a compound formed by one magnesium atom and one nitrogen atom. However, the correct formula for the compound formed by magnesium and nitrogen is Mg3N2.

questions.explanation: Shortcut method: Mg+2 criss-cross charge with N3 to get Mg3N23Mg+ N2 -----> ​=​Mg3​N2​

questions.text: Densities of alkali metals are low due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkali metals have weak forces between their atoms because they are metals and tend to form metallic bonds. These forces are weaker than the covalent or ionic bonds found in other types of compounds.

Alkali metals have relatively large atomic volumes. This is because, as you move down the group in the periodic table, the atomic size increases. The larger the volume occupied by the atoms, the lower the density.
This is incorrect. Alkali metals actually have larger atomic sizes as you move down the group. Larger atomic sizes contribute to lower density.

The electronic configuration of alkali metals is often ns1, where n is the principal quantum number. This electronic configuration implies that there is only one electron in the outermost shell, which contributes to the metals' chemical reactivity but does not directly influence their density.

questions.explanation: The alkali metals have the largest atomic volume due to less no of protons in their nucleus. Density is inversely proportional to volume, so the greater the volume, the less dense the metal would be.

questions.text: Which of the following has the highest value of electronegativity?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.
The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.
The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.

Elements from the halogen group including F, Cl, Br have pretty high electronegativities. The most electronegative element is Fluorine with a score of 4.0 (the highest possible.) Across from Fluorine we also have N and O with high electronegativities. Electronegativity is basically how much the elements 'want' the electrons. A simple way to think about it is that the closer an element is to Fluorine, the higher its electronegativity. The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.

questions.explanation: Elements from the halogen group including F, Cl, Br have pretty high electronegativities. The most electronegative element is Fluorine with a score of 4.0 (the highest possible.) Across from Fluorine we also have N and O with high electronegativities. Electronegativity is basically how much the elements 'want' the electrons. A simple way to think about it is that the closer an element is to Fluorine, the higher its electronegativity. The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.

questions.text: The alkali metals group lies in the _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: d-block is not the correct answers as the d-block consists of transition metals.

The s-block in the periodic table of elements occupies the alkali metals and alkaline earth metals, also known as groups 1 and 2. Helium is also part of the s-block. The principal quantum number "n" fills the s orbital. There is a maximum of two electrons that can occupy the s orbital.
f-block is not the correct answers as the f-block consists of inner transition metals
p-block is not the correct answer as the p-block includes non-metals, metalloids, and some metals.

questions.explanation: The s-block in the periodic table of elements occupies the alkali metals and alkaline earth metals, also known as groups 1 and 2. Helium is also part of the s-block. The principal quantum number “n” fills the s orbital. There is a maximum of two electrons that can occupy the s orbital. A) d-block, C) f-block, and D) p-block are not the correct answers as the d-block consists of transition metals, the f-block consists of inner transition metals, and the p-block includes non-metals, metalloids, and some metals.

questions.text: BeO is _ in nature.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Because BeO is insoluble in pure water but reacts with either an acid (like a base would) or a base (like an acid would), we say that it is amphoteric. All group 1 and 2 oxides are basic in nature except for BeO. Beryllium oxide (BeO) is an amphoteric oxide of group II.

Because BeO is insoluble in pure water but reacts with either an acid (like a base would) or a base (like an acid would), we say that it is amphoteric. All group 1 and 2 oxides are basic in nature except for BeO. Beryllium oxide (BeO) is an amphoteric oxide of group II.

Because BeO is insoluble in pure water but reacts with either an acid (like a base would) or a base (like an acid would), we say that it is amphoteric. All group 1 and 2 oxides are basic in nature except for BeO. Beryllium oxide (BeO) is an amphoteric oxide of group II.
Beryllium oxide (BeO) is an amphoteric oxide of group II.

questions.explanation: Because BeO is insoluble in pure water but reacts with either an acid (like a base would) or a base (like an acid would), we say that it is amphoteric. All group 1 and 2 oxides are basic in nature except for BeO. Beryllium oxide (BeO) is an amphoteric oxide of group II.

questions.text: Metals belonging to the same group in the periodic table are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Mg is in group 2 and Na is in group 1.
Mg is in group 2 and Cu is in group 11.
Mg and Ba are in group 2, so they are in the same group.
Mg is in group 2 and K is in group 1.

questions.explanation: Refer to the image below. As we can see in the periodic table, Magnesium and Barium are the only two metals from the options which are in the same group (Group II).

questions.text: The carbonates of alkali metals are soluble in water except:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Potassium Carbonate's interaction with water is a highly violent and exothermic process in which potassium carbonate is split into potassium and carbonate ions. Potassium Hydroxide and Carbonic Acid are formed when the ions interact.
Lithium carbonate is insoluble in water. Hence, Option B is correct.
When dissolved in water, sodium carbonate forms carbonic acid and sodium hydroxide. As a strong base, sodium hydroxide neutralizes gastric acid thereby acting as an antacid.
Rubidium Carbonate (Rb2CO3) is white in the form of powder. It is generally stable in the air and soluble in water.

questions.explanation: All except Lithium are soluble in water and stable to heat.

questions.text: The flame colour of Ca in flame test is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Flame colors are produced from the movement of the electrons in the metal ions present in the compounds. Flame color of calcium is ORANGE RED. Hence, this option is correct.
Golden yellow is the flame color of sodium.
Red is the flame color of lithium.
Pink is the flame color of potassium.

questions.explanation: The flame of Ca is orange-red so, A is the correct option. A yellow flame is of Sodium (Na), Red flame is of Lithium (Li), and Pink flame is of Potassium (K) thus B, C, and D are incorrect.

questions.text: Which one of the following alkaline earth metal carbonates is thermally the most stable?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: ‘Barium carbonate’ is thermally the most stable. As we move down the group, the charge density of the cation decreases due to increasing cationic radius. As a result, the polarization of the anion decreases, or the polarizing ability of the cation decreases down the group. The lesser the polarization the greater will be the stability of the compound. Hence as we move down the group thermal stability of the carbonates increases. Barium is the lowest down in the group hence its carbonate has the greatest thermal stability.
As we have Be-Mg-Ca-Sr-Ba, hence the stability of Mg is less than Ca, Sr, and Ba.
The stability of Sr is less than Ba.

questions.explanation: 'Barium carbonate' is thermally the most stable. As we move down the group, the charge density of the cation decreases due to increasing cationic radius. As a result, the polarisation of the anion decreases, or the polarising ability of the cation decreases down the group. The lesser the polarisation, the greater the stability of a compound. Hence as we move down the group thermal stability of the carbonates increases. Barium is the lowest down in the group hence its carbonate has the greatest thermal stability.

questions.text: The halogen acid with the highest reducing power is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hydrofluoric acid is the weakest halogen acid in terms of reducing power. It has a lower tendency to donate electrons and act as a reducing agent compared to the other halogen acids. Therefore, it does not have the highest reducing power among the given options.

Reducing power refers to the ability of a substance to reduce another substance by donating electrons. The halogen acids, HF, HCl, HBr, and HI, can all act as reducing agents, but the strength of their reducing power increases as we move down the group. Hence, HI is the halogen acid with the highest reducing power.

Hydrobromic acid has a lower reducing power compared to hydroiodic acid but is still stronger than hydrochloric acid. It can donate electrons and act as a reducing agent, but it is not as strong as hydroiodic acid.

Hydrochloric acid is the weakest halogen acid among the given options in terms of reducing power. While it can donate electrons and act as a reducing agent in certain reactions, its reducing power is lower than that of hydrobromic acid and hydroiodic acid.

questions.explanation: Reducing power refers to the ability of a substance to reduce another substance by donating electrons. The halogen acids, HF, HCl, HBr, and HI, can all act as reducing agents, but the strength of their reducing power increases as we move down the group. Hence, HI is the halogen acid with the highest reducing power.

questions.text: Group 1 elements are called?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Group 1 elements include the alkali metals, Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr. The alkali metals are all soft, shiny and metallic when cut. They react easily with water, have low melting points and densities, and are good conductors of electricity. They all have one valence electron that they lose easily to form ions.

questions.text: Those elements, with an electronic configuration of valence shell ns2 np6 show little tendency to react chemically, are called?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The reactivity of lanthanides is generally lower than that of the corresponding alkali and alkaline earth metals. Lanthanides are relatively unreactive with water and oxygen, and they do not readily dissolve in most acids. However, they can react with strong oxidizing agents, such as chlorine or fluorine, to form a variety of compounds

Actinides are highly reactive elements due to their large atomic and ionic radii, high charge densities, and the presence of f-orbitals, which make them more susceptible to oxidation and reduction reactions.
The alkali metals are highly reactive metals, and their reactivity increases from top to bottom of the group

Noble metals include metals such as gold, silver, platinum, and palladium. These metals are characterized by their low chemical reactivity, which is due to the fact that they have a completely filled outermost electron shell

questions.explanation: The reactivity of lanthanides is generally lower than that of the corresponding alkali and alkaline earth metals. Lanthanides are relatively unreactive with water and oxygen, and they do not readily dissolve in most acids. However, they can react with strong oxidizing agents, such as chlorine or fluorine, to form a variety of compounds. Actinides are highly reactive elements due to their large atomic and ionic radii, high charge densities, and the presence of f-orbitals, which make them more susceptible to oxidation and reduction reactions. The alkali metals are highly reactive metals, and their reactivity increases from top to bottom of the group. Noble metals include metals such as gold, silver, platinum, and palladium. These metals are characterized by their low chemical reactivity, which is due to the fact that they have a completely filled outermost electron shell.

questions.text: Alkali metals cannot make carbides except:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Cesium (Cs) can indeed form carbides. Alkali metals, including cesium, react with carbon to produce cesium carbide (Cs₂C₂). The formation of carbides is more prominent for cesium compared to other alkali metals.
Sodium (Na) can also form carbides. The reaction of sodium with carbon, typically at elevated temperatures, leads to the formation of sodium carbide (Na₂C₂).
Lithium (Li) can form lithium carbide (Li₂C₂) under certain conditions. However, lithium carbides are less stable compared to those of other alkali metals.
Potassium (K) can form carbides as well. The reaction of potassium with carbon results in the formation of potassium carbide (K₄C₄).

questions.explanation: Li more closely resembles the metals of group 2 than the metals of its own group (1). It is more reactive with carbon, nitrogen and hydrogen and less reactive with water, oxygen and halogens. Li forms a carbide (Li2C2) similar to that of calcium.

questions.text: Which one of the following has the highest ionic character?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Fluorine (F) is highly electronegative, and hydrogen (H) has a lower electronegativity. The electronegativity difference is relatively high, making HF a polar covalent molecule with some ionic character.
Sodium (Na) is a metal, and chlorine (Cl) is a nonmetal. The electronegativity difference is relatively high, indicating a strong ionic character. NaCl is a classic example of an ionic compound.
Chlorine is more electronegative than hydrogen, but the electronegativity difference is not as high as in NaCl. HCl is a polar covalent molecule with some ionic character.
Cesium (Cs) is a metal, and fluorine (F) is a nonmetal. The electronegativity difference is relatively high, indicating a significant ionic character. CsF is an ionic compound.

questions.explanation: CsF has the highest ionic character because Cs is the most electropositive element and Fluorine is the most electronegative element. Hence the bond that would be formed between the most electropositive and the most electronegative elements would be very strong and will have the highest ionic character.

questions.text: In modern periodic table all the elements are arranged in ascending order of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Valency of an element can vary and is not the primary organizing principle in the periodic table.
Initially used, but atomic number is the fundamental property for element arrangement.
The modern periodic table arranges elements in ascending order of atomic number, which uniquely identifies each element.
Important for chemical behavior, but not the primary basis for element arrangement.

questions.explanation: The modern periodic table arranges elements in ascending order of atomic number, as it uniquely identifies each element based on the number of protons. This is the fundamental basis for organizing elements in the periodic table. Valency, atomic mass, and valence electrons are not the primary organizing principles, making them incorrect choices.

questions.text: Which of the following always increases on going from top to bottom in a group?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Electronegativity generally decreases on going from top to bottom in a group due to the increase in atomic size and shielding effect of inner electron shells.

As you move down a group, the outermost electrons are located farther from the nucleus, resulting in weaker attraction between the electrons and the nucleus. This leads to an increase in metallic character, with elements at the bottom of a group more likely to exhibit metallic properties like conductivity, malleability, and ductility.
Oxidizing power generally decreases on going from top to bottom in a group due to the increase in atomic size and shielding effect of inner electron shells.
Tendency to get reduced generally decreases on going from top to bottom in a group due to the increase in atomic size and shielding effect of inner electron shells.

questions.explanation: Option b is correct because as you move down a group in the periodic table, the number of electron shells or energy levels increases. The outermost electrons are located farther from the nucleus, which results in weaker attraction between the electrons and the nucleus. As a result, the outer electrons are more easily lost, leading to an increase in metallic character. Elements at the bottom of a group are more likely to exhibit metallic properties like conductivity, malleability, and ductility compared to elements at the top.Options A, C, and D (Electronegativity, Oxidizing power, and Tendency to get reduced) generally decrease on going from top to bottom in a group due to the increase in atomic size and shielding effect of inner electron shells.

questions.text: All of the following periodic properties increase along a period EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ionization energy generally increases along a period because it becomes harder to remove an electron when the effective nuclear charge is higher.

Option B, 'Atomic size,' is correct because atomic size generally decreases along a period, rather than increasing. This is due to the increasing effective nuclear charge (the positive charge felt by the outermost electrons) as you move from left to right across a period.
Electronegativity also generally increases along a period for the same reason as ionization energy. It measures the ability of an atom to attract electrons towards itself.
Electron affinity tends to increase along a period. It represents the energy change that occurs when an electron is added to a neutral atom to form a negative ion.

questions.explanation: Atomic size is the property that does not increase along a period; instead, it decreases due to the increasing effective nuclear charge. Ionization energy, electronegativity, and electron affinity all generally increase along a period due to various atomic properties.

questions.text: Which of the following ions will have greatest radius?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Al+3 has lost three electrons, decreasing its radius compared to a neutral aluminum atom.
B+3 has lost three electrons, making it smaller than a neutral boron atom.
Mg+2 has lost two electrons, reducing its electron cloud and making it smaller than a neutral magnesium atom.

Na⁺ has the largest radius among these ions. Despite losing an electron, the decrease in nuclear charge is not sufficient to counteract the electron-electron repulsion, resulting in a larger radius compared to the other cations.

questions.explanation: Na⁺ has the largest radius because the decrease in nuclear charge after losing an electron is not enough to offset the electron-electron repulsion in the remaining electrons. This results in a relatively larger radius compared to the other cations. The other options, Al+3, B+3, and Mg+2, all involve ions that have lost electrons, leading to a reduction in radius compared to their neutral atom counterparts.

questions.text: An element having low value of ionization energy and low value of electron affinity is likely to belongs to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. An element with low ionization energy and low electron affinity does not belong to this group.
This option is incorrect. An element with low ionization energy and low electron affinity does not belong to this group.

An element with low ionization energy means it requires relatively little energy to remove an electron from its outer shell, indicating that it is likely a metal. Additionally, an element with low electron affinity means it is not very likely to gain an electron, which is a characteristic of metals. Group IA (Group 1) of the periodic table contains the alkali metals, which have low ionization energies and low electron affinities.
This option is incorrect. An element with low ionization energy and low electron affinity does not belong to this group.

questions.explanation: An element with low ionization energy and low electron affinity is likely a metal, as it requires little energy to lose an electron and is not inclined to gain electrons. Group IA (Group 1) of the periodic table consists of alkali metals which exhibit these characteristics. Group IB and Group VIIIA do not fit the criteria as they do not typically have low ionization energy and low electron affinity.

questions.text: Mg is a metal, but chlorine is a non-metal, even then they show which of the following common property:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because while both magnesium and chlorine can participate in redox reactions, they do not always act as reducing agents.
Option B is incorrect because while both magnesium and chlorine can participate in redox reactions, they do not always act as oxidizing agents.

options.text: Both belong to the same period
options.explanation: Magnesium (Mg) and chlorine (Cl) belong to the same period on the periodic table. In this case, they are both in period 3. Elements in the same period have their outermost electrons in the same principal energy level, which leads to similarities in their chemical behavior. However, their actual chemical properties can be quite different because they belong to different groups (columns) on the periodic table.

options.text: Both belong to the same group
options.explanation: Option D is incorrect because they do not belong to the same group (column) on the periodic table; magnesium is an alkaline earth metal (Group 2), while chlorine is a halogen (Group 17).

questions.explanation: Magnesium (Mg) and chlorine (Cl) belong to the same period on the periodic table, which indicates similarities in their chemical behavior. Elements in the same period have their outermost electrons in the same principal energy level. While they have similarities, their actual chemical properties can differ due to belonging to different groups on the periodic table. Therefore, the correct answer is that both elements belong to the same period.

questions.text: Elements in the same vertical group of the periodic table have same:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Elements in the same vertical group, also known as a column or family, of the periodic table have similar electronic configurations, which means they have the same number of valence electrons. This is because they share the same outermost electron shell configuration, which determines the chemical properties of an element.

Elements in the same group (vertical column) have different atomic numbers. Atomic numbers increase from left to right across a period, so elements in the same group have different atomic numbers but similar chemical properties due to their shared valence electron configuration.

Elements in the same group can have different atomic masses. Atomic mass is the weighted average of the masses of all the isotopes of an element, and different elements in the same group can have different proportions of isotopes.

Atomic volume is not a standard property used to categorize elements in the periodic table. Elements in the same group may have different atomic volumes depending on factors like atomic radius and packing arrangements in solids, so it's not a reliable characteristic for classification.

questions.explanation: Elements in the same vertical group, also known as a column or family, of the periodic table have similar electronic configurations, which means they have the same number of valence electrons. This is because they share the same outermost electron shell configuration, which determines the chemical properties of an element. The atomic number and atomic mass can vary within a group. Option A is correct because elements in the same group have the same number of valence electrons, leading to similar chemical properties. Options B, C, and D are incorrect because elements in the same group can have different atomic numbers, atomic masses, and atomic volumes, respectively.

questions.text: Energy required to remove an electron from the outermost shell of an isolated gaseous atom in its ground state is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is the energy change when an electron is added to an atom to form a negative ion, not the energy required to remove an electron.
This is the energy released when ions form a solid compound, not the energy needed to remove an electron from an isolated gaseous atom.
This term is not standard in chemistry and does not relate to the energy required to remove an electron from an atom.
This is the correct answer as it specifically refers to the energy required to remove an electron from an isolated gaseous atom in its ground state.

questions.explanation: Option D, 'Ionization Energy,' is correct because it specifically refers to the energy required to remove an electron from the outermost shell of an isolated gaseous atom in its ground state. The other options are incorrect because they either relate to different energy processes (like electron affinity and lattice energy) or are not recognized terms in chemistry (like crystal energy).

questions.text: All of the carbonates of alkali metals do not decompose on heating EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is correct. Lithium carbonate does not decompose on heating. It remains stable at high temperatures.
Sodium carbonate does decompose on heating, forming sodium oxide (Na2O) and carbon dioxide (CO2).
Potassium carbonate does decompose on heating, resulting in the formation of potassium oxide (K2O) and carbon dioxide (CO2).
Rubidium carbonate also decomposes on heating, producing rubidium oxide (Rb2O) and carbon dioxide (CO2).

questions.explanation: All alkali metal carbonates are generally stable and do not decompose upon heating, except for lithium carbonate (Li₂CO₃). Upon heating, lithium carbonate decomposes to form lithium oxide (Li₂O) and carbon dioxide (CO₂). This is due to the unique properties of lithium compared to other alkali metals. The other options (Na₂CO₃, K₂CO₃, Rb₂CO₃) decompose on heating, forming their respective oxides and carbon dioxide.

questions.text: Which of the following alkali metals forms peroxide when treated with excess oxygen?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It forms normal oxide.
It forms both normal and peroxide.Na + O2 ----------> Na2O2
It forms superoxide.
It forms superoxides.

questions.explanation: When alkali metals react with excess oxygen, sodium (Na) is the one that forms both normal oxide and peroxide. The reaction can be represented as Na + O2 ----------> Na2O2.Lithium (Li) forms a normal oxide, potassium (K) forms a superoxide, and cesium (Cs) also forms superoxides. Therefore, the correct answer is sodium (Na) in this case.

questions.text: Which one of the following alkali metals forms only normal oxide when it reacts with O2?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Only Lithium burns in the air to form a normal oxide Li2O (white solid). Sodium, Potassium, and Rubidium form both normal oxide and peroxide when they react with oxygen.Li + O2 --->Li2O (Lithium oxide)
Sodium, Potassium, and Rubidium form both normal oxide and peroxide when they react with oxygen.
Sodium, Potassium, and Rubidium form both normal oxide and peroxide when they react with oxygen.
Sodium, Potassium, and Rubidium form both normal oxide and peroxide when they react with oxygen.

questions.explanation: Only Lithium forms a normal oxide Li2O when it reacts with oxygen. This is because Lithium is less reactive compared to Sodium, Potassium, and Rubidium, which form both normal oxide and peroxide when they react with oxygen. Therefore, the correct answer is Lithium.

questions.text: Which of the following periodic properties has no unit?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Electron affinity is the energy change that occurs when an atom gains an electron to form a negatively charged ion. It is typically measured in units of energy per mole, such as kilojoules per mole (kJ/mol) or electron volts (eV).
Electronegativity is the power of an atom to attract shared pair of electrons towards itself in a molecule. It is a dimensionless property and does not have any units.

Ionization potential, also known as ionization energy, is the energy required to remove an electron from an atom to form a positive ion (cation). It is typically measured in units of energy per mole, such as kilojoules per mole (kJ/mol) or electron volts (eV).

Hydration energy is the energy released when gaseous ions are dissolved in water to form hydrated ions. It is typically measured in units of energy per mole, such as kilojoules per mole (kJ/mol) or electron volts (eV).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B) Electronegativity.Electronegativity is a dimensionless property and does not have any units. Unlike the other properties listed (electron affinity, ionization potential, and hydration energy), electronegativity is not measured with units of energy per mole.In this context, electron affinity, ionization potential, and hydration energy are all properties that involve energy changes and are measured in units of energy per mole, unlike electronegativity which is a unitless property.

questions.text: Which one of the following elements has greater electronegativity value?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Oxygen (O) is a highly electronegative element with an electronegativity value of 3.44.
Bromine (Br) is electronegative but not as electronegative as fluorine.
Fluorine (F) is the most electronegative element with an electronegativity value of 3.98.
Carbon (C) has a relatively low electronegativity value compared to other elements.

questions.explanation: Fluorine has the highest electronegativity value of 3.98, making it the correct answer. Electronegativity is the ability of an atom to attract bonding electrons. Oxygen, bromine, and carbon have lower electronegativity values compared to fluorine.

questions.text: Pick the correct option.
questions.subject: English

options.text: No star is brighter than the moon.
options.explanation: This sentence is correct because it follows proper comparative structure: "No [noun] is brighter than [another noun]." It clearly states that the moon’s brightness exceeds that of any star.

options.text: No star is more bright than the moon.
options.explanation: It’s wrong because “more bright” is not the standard comparative form of “bright.” The correct comparative is “brighter”, so it should be: “No star is brighter than the moon.”

options.text: No star is brighter then the moon.
options.explanation: It’s incorrect because “then” is used for time or sequence, not comparison. The correct word for comparison is “than”, so it should be: “No star is brighter than the moon.”

options.text: No star is brighter than moon.
options.explanation: It’s incorrect because “the” is missing before “moon.” We need the definite article when referring to a specific celestial object: “No star is brighter than the moon.”

questions.explanation: Option A is correct because it follows proper comparative structure: "No [noun] is brighter than [another noun]." It clearly states that the moon’s brightness exceeds that of any star.

questions.text: Choose the sentence with correct punctuation:
questions.subject: English

options.text: However, it didn't take long for others to jump on board.
options.explanation: This is the correct sentence. The comma after "However" indicates a pause and properly separates the introductory element from the main clause.

options.text: However it didn't take long for others to jump on board.
options.explanation: This sentence is incorrect because it lacks a comma after the introductory word "However" which is necessary for correct punctuation.

options.text: However, it didn't take long for others to jump on board?
options.explanation: This sentence is incorrect due to the use of a question mark, which is inappropriate in the sentence.

options.text: However. It didn't take long for others to jump on board.
options.explanation: This sentence is incorrect because a period is used instead of a comma after "However" which disrupts the flow of the sentence.

questions.explanation: The correct option is: "However, it didn't take long for others to jump on board." A comma is necessary after the introductory word "However" to create a pause and connect it properly to the main clause.

questions.text: "Read the following statement, assuming everything in it to be true. Then decide which of the given suggested courses of action logically follow and are worth pursuing.Statement: Employees are arriving to work more than 5 minutes late every day.Courses of Action:I. Impose a strict penalty of half a day's pay every time someone is late.II. Apply a 15 minutes' buffer. Anyone who is late more than 2 times a month after this buffer period gets docked one day's pay."
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Course of Action I: Impose a strict penalty of half a day's pay every time someone is late.This action may seem extreme and could lead to resentment among employees. It might also discourage a positive work environment. Instead of motivating employees to arrive on time, it could result in decreased morale and productivity. Therefore, this course of action does not logically follow and may not be worth pursuing.Course of Action II: Apply a 15 minutes' buffer. Anyone who is late more than 2 times a month after this buffer period gets docked one day's pay.This action provides a more reasonable approach by allowing a grace period for employees while still holding them accountable for repeated tardiness. Implementing a buffer can help accommodate unforeseen circumstances, and the penalty for exceeding the limit is structured to encourage punctuality without being overly harsh. This course of action logically follows and is worth pursuing.

Implementing a buffer can help accommodate unforeseen circumstances, and the penalty for exceeding the limit is structured to encourage punctuality without being overly harsh. This course of action logically follows and is worth pursuing.

questions.text: Verbal reasoningStatements(I) There is a sharp decline in the production of oilseeds this year.(II) The Government has decided to increase the import quantum of edible oil.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and II is its effect
options.explanation: Statement I is the cause and statement II is it’s effect because firstly there was a massive decline in the production of oilseeds due to which the government decided to import oil Thus only option A is correct

options.text: Statement II is the cause and I is its effect
options.explanation: this option is not suitable.Statement II is the cause and I is its effect: This is incorrect because the government's decision is a response to the decline in oilseed production, not the other way around.

options.text: Both statements are of independent causes
options.explanation: this option is not suitable. Both statements are of independent causes: This is incorrect because the statements are related. The decline in oilseed production directly influences the government's import decision.

options.text: Both statements are the effects of the independent causes
options.explanation: this option is not suitable. Both statements are the effects of the independent causes: This is incorrect as Statement I is not an effect, it is a cause.

questions.explanation: Statement I is the cause and statement II is it’s effect because firstly there was a massive decline in the production of oilseeds due to which the government decided to import oil Thus only option A is correct

questions.text: A ball is a sphere. All spheres are round. Some round things are mesmerizing.Which of the following conclusions is NECESSARILY TRUE?Conclusions:I. All balls are mesmerizing.II. All round things are spheres.III. Some mesmerizing things are spheres.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Since, all spheres are round and some round things are mesmerizing, then conclusion III, "Some mesmerizing things are spheres" is necessarily true.
Conclusion I cannot be true, because some balls can be mesmerizing, not all. Conclusion II cannot be true because the given statements do tell if all round things are spheres.
Conclusion II cannot be true because the given statements do tell if all round things are spheres.
Conclusion I cannot be true, because some balls can be mesmerizing, not all.

questions.explanation: I. "All balls are mesmerizing" is not necessarily true. The statements provided do not establish a direct connection between all balls and being mesmerizing. So, this conclusion cannot be necessarily true.II. "All round things are spheres" is not necessarily true either. While it is true that all spheres are round, the statements do not provide enough information to conclude that all round things are spheres. There can be other objects that are round but not spherical.III. "Some mesmerizing things are spheres" is necessarily true. The statements establish that "Some round things are mesmerizing," and since all spheres are round (as stated in the first premise), it is necessarily true that some mesmerizing things are spheres.So, the correct conclusion is III, making option A the correct choice.
